user_message: I need to run the hygiene check for the Quantum Analytics engagement.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 12:07 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Quantum Analytics

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 10:35 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 16:08 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Quantum Analytics
Date: 2024-03-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:13:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:18:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:25:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:30:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Quantum Analytics: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
